children.heading: Research on causes and consequences of childhood hunger
children.label: a Research on causes and consequences of childhood hunger
A
children.text: the causes of childhood hunger and food insecurity;

children.label: B
children.text: the characteristics of households with childhood hunger and food insecurity; and

children.label: C
children.text: the consequences of childhood hunger and food insecurity.

children.label: A
children.text: enter into competitively awarded contracts or cooperative agreements; or

children.label: B
children.text: provide grants to States or public or private agencies or organizations, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Application
children.label: 3 Application
children.text: To be eligible to enter into a contract or cooperative agreement or receive a grant under this subsection, a State or public or private agency or organization shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary shall require.

children.label: A
children.text: economic, health, social, cultural, demographic, and other factors that contribute to childhood hunger or food insecurity;

children.label: B
children.text: the geographic distribution of childhood hunger and food insecurity;

children.label: i
children.text: existing Federal assistance programs, including title 26, reduce childhood hunger and food insecurity; and

children.label: I
children.text: gaps in program coverage;

children.label: II
children.text: the inability of potential participants to access programs; or

children.label: III
children.text: the insufficiency of program benefits or services;

children.label: D
children.text: the public health and medical costs of childhood hunger and food insecurity;

children.label: E
children.text: an estimate of the degree to which the Census Bureau measure of food insecurity underestimates childhood hunger and food insecurity because the Census Bureau excludes certain households, such as homeless, or other factors;

children.label: F
children.text: the effects of childhood hunger on child development, well-being, and educational attainment; and

children.label: G
children.text: such other critical outcomes as are determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On , out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer to the Secretary to carry out this subsection $10,000,000, to remain available until expended.October 1, 2012

children.heading: Receipt and acceptance
children.label: B Receipt and acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall be entitled to receive, shall accept, and shall use to carry out this subsection the funds transferred under subparagraph (A), without further appropriation.

children.heading: Demonstration projects to end childhood hunger
children.label: b Demonstration projects to end childhood hunger

children.heading: Child
children.label: A Child
children.text: The term “child” means a person under the age of 18.

children.heading: Supplemental nutrition assistance program
children.label: B Supplemental nutrition assistance program
children.text: The term “supplemental nutrition assistance program” means the supplemental nutrition assistance program established under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 2011

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: Under such terms and conditions as are established by the Secretary, the Secretary shall carry out demonstration projects that test innovative strategies to end childhood hunger, including alternative models for service delivery and benefit levels that promote the reduction or elimination of childhood hunger and food insecurity.

children.label: A
children.text: enhance benefits provided under the supplemental nutrition assistance program for eligible households with children;

children.label: B
children.text: enhance benefits or provide for innovative program delivery models in the school meals, afterschool snack, and child and adult care food programs under this chapter and the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 ( et seq.); and42 U.S.C. 1771

children.label: C
children.text: target Federal, State, or local assistance, including emergency housing or family preservation services, at households with children who are experiencing hunger or food insecurity, to the extent permitted by the legal authority establishing those assistance programs and services.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary may enter into competitively awarded contracts or cooperative agreements with, or provide grants to, public or private organizations or agencies (as determined by the Secretary), for use in accordance with demonstration projects that meet the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: ii Requirement
children.text: At least 1 demonstration project funded under this subsection shall be carried out on an Indian reservation in a rural area with a service population with a prevalence of diabetes that exceeds 15 percent, as determined by the Director of the Indian Health Service.

children.heading: Application
children.label: B Application
children.text: To be eligible to receive a contract, cooperative agreement, or grant under this subsection, an organization or agency shall submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: i
children.text: an identification of a low-income target group that reflects individuals experiencing hunger or food insecurity;

children.label: ii
children.text: a commitment to a demonstration project that allows for a rigorous outcome evaluation as described in paragraph (6);

children.label: iii
children.text: a focus on innovative strategies to reduce the risk of childhood hunger or provide a significant improvement to the food security status of households with children; and

children.label: iv
children.text: such other criteria as are determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Health and Human Services;

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of Labor; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.label: i
children.text: measures the impact of each demonstration project on appropriate participation, food security, nutrition, and associated behavioral outcomes among participating households; and

children.label: ii
children.text: uses rigorous experimental designs and methodologies, particularly random assignment or other methods that are capable of producing scientifically valid information regarding which activities are effective in reducing the prevalence or preventing the incidence of food insecurity and hunger in the community, especially among children.

children.label: I
children.text: the status of each demonstration project; and

children.label: II
children.text: the results of any evaluations of the demonstration projects completed during the previous fiscal year; and

children.label: ii
children.text: ensure that the evaluation results are shared broadly to inform policy makers, service providers, other partners, and the public in order to promote the wide use of successful strategies.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On , out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the Secretary of the Treasury shall transfer to the Secretary to carry out this subsection $40,000,000, to remain available until .October 1, 2012September 30, 2017

children.heading: Receipt and acceptance
children.label: B Receipt and acceptance
children.text: The Secretary shall be entitled to receive, shall accept, and shall use to carry out this subsection the funds transferred under subparagraph (A), without further appropriation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Funds made available under subparagraph (A) may be used to carry out this subsection, including to pay Federal costs associated with developing, soliciting, awarding, monitoring, evaluating, and disseminating the results of each demonstration project under this subsection.

children.heading: Indian reservations
children.label: ii Indian reservations
children.text: Of amounts made available under subparagraph (A), the Secretary shall use a portion of the amounts to carry out research relating to hunger, obesity and type 2 diabetes on Indian reservations, including research to determine the manner in which Federal nutrition programs can help to overcome those problems.

children.label: I
children.text: describes the manner in which Federal nutrition programs can help to overcome child hunger nutrition problems on Indian reservations; and

children.label: II
children.text: contains proposed administrative and legislative recommendations to strengthen and streamline all relevant Department of Agriculture nutrition programs to reduce childhood hunger, obesity, and type 2 diabetes on Indian reservations.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: i Duration
children.text: No project may be funded under this subsection for more than 5 years.

children.heading: Project requirements
children.label: ii Project requirements
children.text: No project that makes use of, alters, or coordinates with the supplemental nutrition assistance program may be funded under this subsection unless the project is fully consistent with the project requirements described in section 17(b)(1)(B) of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ().7 U.S.C. 2026(b)(1)(B)

children.heading: Hunger-free communities
children.label: iii Hunger-free communities
children.text: No project may be funded under this subsection that receives funding under .section 7517 of title 71See References in Text note below.1

children.label: I
children.text: this chapter;

children.label: II
children.text: the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 ( et seq.);42 U.S.C. 1771

children.label: III
children.text: the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.); or7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: IV
children.text: the Emergency Food Assistance Act of 1983 ( et seq.).7 U.S.C. 7501
